stem: A food manufacturing study measures the weight (grams) for a sample of 15 cereal boxes. The sample mean is 222.8 g with a sample standard deviation of 63.7 g. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 211.1 g and standard deviation of 43.9 g. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 222.8, 95% CI = [187.5, 258.1]. Without outliers: mean = 211.1, 95% CI = [185.8, 236.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [185.8, 258.1]. Point estimate: 216.9 g.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [185.8, 258.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
